grades.expectation: Response identifies 68 years as a critical lease length issue
grades.reason: Response clearly identifies 68-year lease as a 'significant concern' and 'critical issue'

grades.expectation: Response mentions specific lender names and their minimum requirements (not just 'most lenders want 70+ years')
grades.reason: Response only mentions 'high street lenders' and 'specialist lenders' without naming specific institutions

grades.expectation: Response mentions marriage value applies below 80 years
grades.reason: Response explicitly states 'Below 80 years, marriage value applies to lease extensions'

grades.expectation: Response discusses lease extension options (statutory Section 42, informal, assign benefit)
grades.reason: Response mentions Section 42 notice, informal negotiation with freeholder, and collective enfranchisement

grades.expectation: Response quantifies the price impact (discount percentage or range)
grades.reason: Response mentions buyers will need £15k-40k+ for extension but doesn't quantify the actual discount percentage or range on property value

grades.expectation: Response mentions reduced buyer pool due to mortgage restrictions
grades.reason: Response clearly states this 'severely limits your buyer pool to cash purchasers or specialist lenders'
